Acceptance Oracle: Architecture diagram, generated process graph, and source tree confirm the four declared process boundaries and interfaces.
Dependency rules deny inference clients in Rust, deny canonical matching-state mutation from Python, and prove that cross-process mutations use versioned IPC only.
Replay denies AF_INET, AF_INET6, and DNS while permitted AF_UNIX IPC succeeds; prohibited access aborts the run and commits no valid final artifact.
Only allowlisted non-transactional research data routes succeed; unknown destinations fail and produce durable network-denial records.
Source, dependency, configuration, secret, and network-policy scans find no remote execution adapter, private-key path, payment path, or real-service submission route.
An allowed peer with an invalid semantic payload receives the correct rejection and cannot bypass Rust-side freshness, target-version, bounds, or replay validation.
A connection from a mismatched effective UID/GID or unsupported user-namespace mapping is rejected fail-closed.
Gateway binds only local interfaces, resolves no external host, and supports the complete local mock lifecycle contract.
Any environment other than LOCAL_MOCK_DEMO, or any configuration containing an external hostname or credential, is rejected before startup.
Identical intents routed to local matching and mock gateway produce records with distinct execution classes and cannot be merged silently.
A scripted rejection, partial fill, cancellation, and final fill produce complete canonical audit records with matching hashes and timestamps.
A frozen mock trace replays with no gateway call and yields identical canonical lifecycle artifacts.
Controlled fixtures reproduce precomputed disagreement, latency, quantity, price, and lifecycle-divergence metrics.
Identical trace under throttled and unthrottled CPU load yields identical logical timestamps, dispositions, ledgers, and canonical state hash; runtime telemetry may differ.
A historically dated correction first observed after decision_cutoff_at does not alter any earlier materialized forecast artifact.
Version vectors at or below DeltaSkewMax are accepted; vectors above it are deterministically rejected as incoherent.
Telemetry labels RTT and local durations correctly and contains no subtraction of unrelated process monotonic timestamps.
High-frequency readers during concurrent snapshot/delta publication observe no torn state and exactly match a serial reference model.
A gap or reconnect marks the market Fragmented and blocks all dependent operations until authoritative snapshot and eligible deltas are atomically validated.
Invalid or stale states return RejectedStateInvalid; valid states with inadequate depth return RejectedInsufficientDepth; sufficient states match the reference depth calculation.
Boundary fuzzing produces no unchecked wraparound or process panic; out-of-range operations return the documented error without state mutation.
Wide-intermediate notional calculations match an independent high-precision reference within the configured maximum scaled-unit rounding error.
Negative, above-scale, malformed, and inconsistent uncertainty values are rejected with RejectedBounds before policy evaluation.
A reference scenario with sufficient depth but insufficient cash after costs is rejected without changing visible depth or ledger state.
Oversized and incomplete frames preserve bounded memory; no payload allocation proportional to the declared oversized length occurs; connection closes at the configured limit or timeout.
ReceiptStatus and DispositionStatus deserialize only declared variants; AcceptedQueued precedes later lifecycle disposition and cannot be mistaken for completion.
Omission or invalidity of any required field deterministically returns RejectedSchema or RejectedBounds with no queued message.
When the incoming message ranks below all retained messages, it receives RejectedCapacity and queue contents remain exactly unchanged.
When the incoming message outranks a known queued message, it receives AcceptedQueued, the expected message receives Evicted, and the final retained ID set exactly matches the fixture.
A legitimate retry preserves identity and returns DuplicateRetry after journal commit; a new process instance is accepted; sequence regression in an existing instance returns ReplaySequenceViolation.
Crash injection after each journal lifecycle boundary reconstructs the unique last durable state and validates checksums and hash linkage.
Crash before durable journaling followed by retry produces exactly one terminal disposition.
Crash after durable journaling followed by retry returns DuplicateRetry and produces no additional disposition.
With database unavailable and spool capacity available, every record is checksummed, spooled, and reconciled exactly once.
When spool capacity is exhausted, no new state-changing work is accepted, the system enters the configured Degraded or Halting state, and no record is silently deleted.
SIGKILL before plan, after plan, during apply, after apply, and after commit results in exactly one final ledger transition for the transition ID.
A policy or model-affecting configuration change after holdout access is rejected unless a new experiment ID and untouched evaluation period are registered.
Identical canonical inputs produce a bit-identical intent and deterministic simulation_intent_id; random runtime metadata is excluded from the canonical hash.
The preregistration manifest contains every required model, policy, metric, baseline, matching, cost, latency, exclusion, and correction field before evaluation.
An unregistered holdout request is rejected and durably recorded; any later design change requires a new experiment.
No underlying_event_group_id crosses partitions, and the declared frozen or prequential protocol is applied without mixing.
Architecture review confirms that raw model outputs cannot reach calibrated_probability without a separately versioned, temporally eligible calibration artifact.
With raw output fixed, changing the valid calibration artifact changes only calibrated fields and legitimate downstream artifacts; missing calibration triggers abstention or preregistered fallback.
Every forecast in the causal historical primary evaluation references a frozen model artifact whose documented cutoff precedes the forecast cutoff; ineligible forecasts are excluded and reported.
Valid two-sided, one-sided, stale, fragmented, missing, and wide-spread fixtures apply the preregistered baseline or exclusion policy exactly.
Mutating every market update strictly after t_arrival leaves the arrival-state fill decision bit-identical.
A multi-level reference book reproduces exact all-or-none status, weighted notional, limit enforcement, cost check, and atomic reservation.
Confirmed trades, unclassified cancellations, partial fills, and delayed cancellation reproduce the exact conservative queue lifecycle and unobservable cases.
Concurrent intents cannot consume more than shared virtual depth or cash, and all declared ledger invariants remain true.
Identical arrival timestamps produce the exact preregistered total order across repeated runs.
Disputed status produces no settlement; Final Yes/No, Void, Cancelled, Invalid, and DefinitionChanged apply the preregistered ledger and scoring policy.
Results are segmented by all preregistered participation-ratio bands and clearly state the no-market-impact assumption.
A controlled fixture reproduces Brier Score, clipped Log Loss, calibration diagnostics, coverage, abstention, and skill against both baselines.
Multiple forecasts for one event-group/horizon yield exactly the preregistered primary forecast or aggregation, independent of eventual outcome.
A grouped fixture with fixed configuration reproduces reference clustered intervals within EpsilonMetric.
A controlled reference ledger reproduces every declared P&L, cost, fill, slippage, markout, drawdown, and inventory metric within specified tolerances.
A controlled abstention fixture reproduces covered-subset coverage and score and all-event baseline-fallback score.
Empty, one-sided, stale, fragmented, partially liquid, and fully liquid fixtures produce the exact declared valuation status and no silent optimistic value.
A valid offline replay with frozen artifacts produces bit-identical canonical selections, dispositions, ledgers, and final state hashes across repeated runs.
A prohibited external call aborts the run, records the violation, marks the run invalid, and produces no valid final research artifact.
The fixed attack corpus remains bounded, cannot bypass schema validation, executes no content, and produces complete robustness metrics.

The document validator confirms unique normative IDs, unique verification IDs, matching names, no invisible controlled-identifier characters, and at least one verification or reviewed justification per requirement.
